current_active_queue.label: Real SDK Vertical Slice And Canonical Runtime Evidence
current_active_queue.why_now: Local AI Runtime keeps the same active queue after the strategic return. The repo now accepts compliant AgentBridge markdown task intake on host_local through a fail-closed adapter, completes a repo-side projection parity loop from markdown task to result.json plus markdown projection, and still keeps result.json as formal evidence with AgentBridge markdown as compatibility projection. With host_local crash recovery, lease helpers, exec fallback process-guard, the minimal verification runner, the safe intake adapter, repo-side parity, the repo-side planner handoff, the repo-side review gate, repo-side predicate coverage plus force-on overrides, the git-backed write-boundary enforcement, the minimal worktree manager, the cleanup manager, and the runtime-backed `dispatch_state.json` ledger now landed, the next bounded execution sequence moves from ledger materialization to lifecycle operations (`stale / cancelled / resumed / retry`) plus structured review/closeout receipts while the selector stays at promote_phase1_execution.

current_live_posture.summary: Local AI Runtime currently executes repo-side through canonical normalization on host_local, writes result.json as formal evidence, writes `.ai/runs/<run_id>/<task_id>/dispatch_state.json` as the runtime ledger companion, and keeps AgentBridge results as compatibility projection. The execution hot path is Codex-first on `host_local`, while Hermes stays in the repo truth as the risk/ledger/cross-executor sidecar and Claude remains an optional review sidecar that is not yet live-wired. host_local crash recovery, minimal lease lifecycle helpers, exec fallback process-tree guarding, the minimal verification runner, a fail-closed AgentBridge markdown intake adapter, repo-side projection parity, a repo-side planner handoff gate, a repo-side review gate, repo-side predicate coverage plus force-on overrides, git-backed write-boundary enforcement, a minimal worktree manager, a cleanup manager, and the runtime-backed dispatch ledger are now landed. Planner-gated tasks currently stop at `waiting_handoff` before any live planner or primary worker execution; review-gated tasks currently stop at `needs_review` after worker plus verification and before downstream flow; low-risk tasks can now complete without an automatic blocking review, while medium/high/critical risk, policy-surface changes, and force-on review continue to stop the run. Declared isolated worktree tasks now fail closed on repo-escape path claims, worktree root drift, branch drift, and post-run writes outside `allowed_paths` or inside `forbidden_paths` when the workspace has a `.git` admin path; worktree isolation currently covers writes only and does not imply memory/provider/session isolation. Runtime-managed clean linked worktrees now auto-remove after a successful non-handoff run, while review-pending, failed, dirty, or externally launched isolated worktrees stay deferred with an auditable `worktree_cleanup` event, and unexpected remove errors surface as `cleanup_failed`. `result.json` now carries `cleanup_owner`, `status_reason`, and `dispatch_state_ref`, while `runtime_tasks` indexes `run_id / attempt / state_reason / next_action / cleanup_status / cleanup_owner / dispatch_state_path`. Branch deletion still remains out of scope, and the schema now reserves `queued / input_required / cancelled / stale / resumed` even though lifecycle operations are not fully wired yet. `user_forced_planner / user_forced_review` now act as force-on-only overrides rather than documentation-only fields. Hermes -> AgentBridge -> Codex remains the product mainline and migration target; non-host_local runners are still not wired, execution-critical markdown overrides remain intentionally unsupported, and these repo-side planner/review/path-guard/worktree/cleanup/ledger claims do not yet mean live `Direct GPT-5.4` planner, live `Claude Code + GLM-5.2` review, platform compatibility green, or live accepted. The GPT-5.4 gateway probe and the codex exec minimum probe are ready; network_proxy remains platform_na, so current live execution should still stay within pure local task boundaries.

certified_baseline.label: Hermes AgentBridge Historical Baseline And Strategic Reference
certified_baseline.claim_boundary: Hermes/AgentBridge remains the certified historical baseline and strategic north-star reference for Local AI Runtime as a three-layer product line. Current repo runtime truth stays on canonical internal normalization plus result.json evidence and compatibility projection, with direct host_local markdown intake now wired under repo-owned defaults and fail-closed override rejection.
